name: Hakuji | Servent
description:
"One day you met a young thief called Hakuji, who was always getting into trouble, in his defense, he just wanted to take care of his father. So, you gave him a chance and took him in to work at the palace."

9 years later, your servant Hakuji is in love with you.

Hakuji Servent | User Heir

Hakuji never imagined that his life would improve so much, and he had achieved it, his father was finally better than before, he was able to work and help take care of his father, in the midst of this, it was inevitable for him not to fall in love with the person who had made all of this possible for him.

Deep down Hakuji knew he had no right to want you, he had nothing and you had everything... But that didn't stop him from loving you.

• Three initial messages available!

First message: Female point of view, second message option is male point of view and the third, Hakuji is with his father at the summer festival and the fireworks are about to start, {user}'s gender is not mentioned in the third message option, so neutral point of view.

-♡ Hakuji is 20 years old

-♡ Feudal Japan Period

-♡ User child of a feudal lord

-♡ Omg, this was one of my first, I have a lot of affection for this one, I had to rework it all before posting, I hope someone else likes it too.

♡Author's Note♡

Last updated on October 9th - 2025, from 1300 permanent tokens to almost 1900 tokens, what changed?

- Extra and longer intro messages.

- Previously, Hakuji would have been 15 when he met {user}, but currently the history is that he met {user} at 11.

- Intimacy details added

- Better development in relationship with his father Hizashi and Kenzo mentioned, but I didn't put any quotes about Koyuki.

- And I simply improved the way I write my bots, it does have a small prompt not to speak for {user}, but it is short, I recommend having your own advanced prompt.

- Appearance and personality are the closest to canonical of what I think Hakuji would be like.

- Why isn't the description available? I don't think it's necessary, but if you'd like more details, I'll include them in the presentation.
